testo: Gorgias' PTMO argues for three main theses: (1) nothing is; (2) even if it were, it could not be known; (3) if it were and could be known, it could not be communicated to anyone.
note: MENTION testuale delle tre tesi del PTMO. Esposizione strutturale dell'argomento gorgiano, distinta dall'interpretazione metafisica della medesima (cfr. zil_016, zil_018).

testo: Sextus presents Gorgias' claims by following the tripartite scheme (e.g. the trilemma, an argument with three different options on the table), which is typical of his (Sextus') sceptical argumentations.
marker_linguistico: three different options on the table
note: MENTION del trilemma sceptico antico (Sesto/Agrippa: regresso/circolo/anipotesi) come fonte storica diretta del Trilemma di Münchhausen. Albert (1968) ricalca i tre modi di Agrippa. Il trilemma è pattern induttivo di esaustione delle modalità di giustificazione, non strategia confutativa.

testo: If it is unlimited, it is nowhere. For if it is somewhere, then what it is in is different from it, and in this way what is, being enclosed within something, will no longer be unlimited. For what encloses is larger than what is enclosed, while nothing is larger than the unlimited, so that the unlimited is not somewhere.
marker_linguistico: what it is in is different from it
what encloses is larger than what is enclosed
note: MENTION dell'argomento zenoniano del luogo (T4 in Zilioli). Struttura: ogni luogo richiede un luogo ulteriore (regresso). Istanza di C₁_esplicito nella tradizione greca pre-classica.

testo: Since whatever exists must be in a place, if place itself existed it would be in a place, and that place in a further place, and so on and so forth, which is absurd. Therefore place does not exist. But if anything existed it would be in a place. Therefore nothing exists.
marker_linguistico: in a further place, and so on and so forth
note: MENTION del C₁_esplicito: regresso del luogo (Zeno → Sedley 2017). Marker 'and so on and so forth' + 'which is absurd' = forma del regresso. Pattern lessicale corrispondente ai marker del REPORT §6.

testo: What comes into existence can be generated either from what is or from what is not. In the former case, it would mean that to generate something different, what is should be by necessity modified and transformed into what is not – something that is not possible. On the other hand, generation from what is not is impossible, because nothing would originate from nothing.
marker_linguistico: either from what is or from what is not
note: MENTION dell'argomento confutativo gorgiano. Struttura: esaustione dilemmatica delle opzioni eleatiche. Non istanzia un corno specifico del Trilemma; è un metodo confutativo che sfrutta una premessa eleatica (cfr. zil_007 omogeneità del being).

testo: If it is, it is one or more. But if it is neither one nor many, then it would be nothing. [...] For if it is not one, it is not multiple either: for a plurality is a composition of unities, and that is why, if the one is destroyed, the plurality is destroyed together with it.
marker_linguistico: plurality is a composition of unities
note: MENTION dell'argomento gorgiano contro monismo/pluralismo. Stessa struttura confutativa dilemmatica di zil_005. La presupposizione di esaustività (one xor many) è premessa logica eleatica usata in funzione confutativa.

testo: This argument rests on the Eleatic assumption that being is fully and completely homogeneous, that is, always identical to itself and undergoing no ontological change whatsoever.
note: MENTION di C₃ dogmatico nascosto della posizione eleatica: l'omogeneità del being è postulato non-derivato. Zilioli lo identifica come 'assumption' — marker canonico del C₃ dogmatico. Target: posizione_filosofica eleatica.

testo: It is not correct to say that intrinsic nature is produced by means of causes and conditions. An intrinsic nature that was produced by causes and conditions would be a product. But how could there ever be an intrinsic nature that is a product? For intrinsic nature is not adventitious, nor is it dependent on something else.
note: MENTION dell'argomento di Nāgārjuna CONTRO l'essenzialismo (svabhāva). Struttura confutativa semantica: svabhāva è per definizione indipendente, quindi incompatibile con causation. Nāgārjuna NON istanzia svabhāva — argomenta contro chi lo postula. Target: Nāgārjuna come autore dell'argomento confutativo.

testo: Not from itself, not from another, not from both, nor without cause: Never in any way is there any existing thing that has arisen. [...] According to these celebrated verses, there are four possible logical ways in which existing things may be thought to be caused/arisen [...]. Nāgārjuna rejects all these four possibilities.
marker_linguistico: Not from itself, not from another
note: MENTION del catuṣkoṭi (tetralemma) nagarjuniano: strategia confutativa che nega tutte e quattro le opzioni logiche disponibili per ridurre l'avversario all'assurdo. Da distinguere dal trilemma di Sesto (zil_002), che è pattern induttivo di esaustione delle modalità di giustificazione, non strategia confutativa.

testo: If something existed by essential nature, then there would not be the nonexistence of such a thing. For it never holds that there is the alteration of essential nature. [...] If there were existence in itself of things, there would be eternalism; if there is non-being, there is necessarily annihilation.
note: MENTION (via Nāgārjuna) di C₃ modale mascherato della posizione eternalista/essenzialista. Modali 'would not be', 'necessarily' = ponti non dedotti. Nāgārjuna argomenta contro questa posizione, non la istanzia. Target: posizione_filosofica essenzialista.

testo: No [atom] is simple, being many-sided; no atom is sideless (nāpradeśaś) [in so far as its connection with other atoms would, then, be impossible]; on the other hand, the idea of plurality is inconceivable without that of unity nor that of non-existence without that of existence.
note: MENTION dell'argomento Vasubandhu/Yogācārin contro l'atomismo. Struttura: dilemma mereologico sugli atomi. Confutazione di una posizione ontologica, non istanza di corno.

testo: What exists by intrinsic essence – Nāgārjuna claims – cannot change in any respect and at any time; if age is something to be had by svabhāva, a young man could never become old: It is not correct to say that change pertains to the thing itself that is said to change or to what is distinct. For a youth does not age, nor does the aged one age.
note: MENTION (via Nāgārjuna) di C₃ dogmatico della posizione essenzialista: svabhāva implica immutabilità. Nāgārjuna ne fa una reductio per CONFUTARE l'essenzialismo, non per istanziarlo. Target: posizione_filosofica essenzialista. Borderline tra C₃ dogmatico e modale mascherato.

testo: It could not be true, the opponent argues, that all things are empty. If this were the case, there would be nothing that is empty, that is, nihilism would be true. To which objection Nāgārjuna replies that there could not be any change if there were things with intrinsic nature.
marker_linguistico: there would be nothing that is empty
note: MENTION dell'obiezione opponentista (lettura nichilistica): se tutto è vuoto, la tesi della vacuità si auto-svuota. Target: lettura_interpretativa contestata, NON posizione di Nāgārjuna (che la rifiuta in MMK 24:18). La struttura INCL_auto_referenza si applica all'interpretazione contestata.

testo: Nāgārjuna says he has no views to defend. It is also true that, in a sort of (proto-)Wittgensteinian fashion, Nāgārjuna attempts to defend the special status of his statement about emptiness in a crucial section of the Vigrahavyāvartanī, hence throwing away the ladder after climbing on it (see Vigrahavyāvartanī 24). But I am not sure that his attempt is successful and cannot be charged with circularity.
note: MENTION della charge di circolarità formulata da Zilioli contro Nāgārjuna (VV 24, mossa 'wittgensteiniana' di throwing away the ladder). Marker 'I am not sure' + 'charged with circularity'. Target: lettura_interpretativa. La fondatezza della charge va fuori scopo dell'annotazione.

testo: Pyrrho committed himself to at least one metaphysical position about things in the world, namely that things (ta pragmata) are 'undifferentiated, unstable and indeterminate (adiáphora kaì astáthmēta kaì anepíkrita)'. Pyrrho's indeterminacy thesis is not nihilism, but it is something remarkably close to it.
note: MENTION dell'interpretazione di Bett/Beckwith su Pirrone come dotato di tesi metafisica positiva sotto apparente scetticismo. Marker 'committed himself to' + 'metaphysical position'. Target: lettura_interpretativa.

testo: The paper ends with a hermeneutical suggestion: that is, to replace traditional 'sceptical' interpretations of Gorgias and Nāgārjuna with an alternative one, which takes them as possibly committed to nihilism. [...] what hermeneutical revision could we make to the history of Greek philosophy if we took Gorgias not only as a simple proposer of nihilist arguments for rhetorical or dialectical purposes but also as a believer in them too?
note: DIAGNOSIS sostantiva di Zilioli: passaggio da 'Gorgia argomenta per nihilismo' a 'Gorgia crede nel nihilismo'. Marker chiave 'believer in them too'. La transizione argomentazione→credenza presuppone assunzioni esplicitate in zil_018.

testo: Westerhoff takes metaphysical nihilism as the combination of ontological eliminativism (as he puts it, 'only the fundamental exists') and non-foundationalism ('it's dependence all the way down').
note: MENTION definitoria. 'Dependence all the way down' è C₁ esplicito (regresso) posto come tesi ontologica positiva. Pattern simmetrico a Klein infinitismo (C₁ con polarità strumentale) in registro ontologico.

testo: What hermeneutical revision could we make to the history of Greek philosophy if we took Gorgias not only as a simple proposer of nihilist arguments for rhetorical or dialectical purposes but also as a believer in them too? In this way, Gorgias could profitably join a nihilist/eliminativist strand in ancient Greek metaphysics.
note: USE C₃ dogmatico nascosto da parte di Zilioli. Il passaggio 'Gorgia argomenta per X' → 'Gorgia crede X' presuppone un postulato non dichiarato di isomorfismo argomentazione→credenza (o linguaggio→mondo). Marker 'proposer of nihilist arguments' + 'believer in them too' = transizione che richiede l'assunzione.
